title: Investigate and patch 'throughput bleed' during regional surges impacting TTFT and tokens/sec across Hosted/Dedicated/Private
preview: Multiple customers across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments reported abrupt TTFT spikes and sustained tokens/sec degradation during regional traffic surges. Symptoms look like a 'throughput bleed' where initial-token latency climbs and steady-state token emission rate drops by 20-60% for affected variants. ...
text: Multiple customers across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments reported abrupt TTFT spikes and sustained tokens/sec degradation during regional traffic surges. Symptoms look like a 'throughput bleed' where initial-token latency climbs and steady-state token emission rate drops by 20-60% for affected variants. This ticket coordinates triage, a short-term patch, and follow-up hardening across runtime scheduler, KV cache policy, and kernel-selection heuristics.

title: Heterogeneous GPU headroom miscount causing silent dedicated shortfalls
preview: Multiple enterprise customers observed silent capacity shortfalls in Reserved Pools which manifested as increased cold-starts and higher p95 latency during burst traffic windows. Initial triage showed autoscaler did not add headroom quickly enough for tenants using GPU variants with lower vCPU equivalence, causing t...
text: Multiple enterprise customers observed silent capacity shortfalls in Reserved Pools which manifested as increased cold-starts and higher p95 latency during burst traffic windows. Initial triage showed autoscaler did not add headroom quickly enough for tenants using GPU variants with lower vCPU equivalence, causing the scheduler to under-allocate across heterogeneous pools. This ticket tracks the investigation, immediate mitigations, hotfix rollout, and follow-up reliability work.

title: Alert onboarding checklist & triage journey mapper for Console
preview: Goal: Design an onboarding checklist and a visual triage journey map inside the Redwood Console to reduce time-to-first-alert and improve on-call handovers. Motivation: new teams onboard to Redwood often miss key alert configuration steps (thresholds, notification routing, runbooks) which leads to noisy alerts, on-c...
text:
Goal: Design an onboarding checklist and a visual triage journey map inside the Redwood Console to reduce time-to-first-alert and improve on-call handovers. Motivation: new teams onboard to Redwood often miss key alert configuration steps (thresholds, notification routing, runbooks) which leads to noisy alerts, on-call confusion, and longer MTTR. This ticket covers a lightweight, stage-based checklist paired with an interactive triage timeline that surfaces runbooks, recent postmortem links, and a one-click ‘handoff snapshot’ for the next shift.

Scope: prototype UI patterns (checklist modal, stepper, contextual help, compact handoff snapshot), annotated copy for microcopy review, and a small usability test with 6 internal SRE/PM volunteers. Excludes backend integration work (alerts API wiring) — that will be a dependency owned by ENG.

Design principles: 1) Progressive disclosure: sh...

title: Admin Action Custody Flow: Data Residency Locking, Retention Policies, and Append-Only Exporter
preview: Deliver an append-only export pipeline and residency-aware custody flow for admin actions (config changes, role grants, access revocations). This ticket covers: (1) a write-once exporter that pushes signed batches into region-scoped WORM buckets, (2) manifest generation with cryptographic custody stamps and retentio...
text: Deliver an append-only export pipeline and residency-aware custody flow for admin actions (config changes, role grants, access revocations). This ticket covers: (1) a write-once exporter that pushes signed batches into region-scoped WORM buckets, (2) manifest generation with cryptographic custody stamps and retention markers, and (3) an automated evidence report that exports compliance-ready bundles for legal/forensic requests. Goal: ensure tamper-evident admin audit data with programmable retention and easy handoff for auditors.

title: Fix inconsistent final-token boundaries after SSE reconnects causing duplicate/missing tokens
preview: Summary: Several customers reported truncated or duplicated tokens at the very end of Server-Sent Events (SSE) streams after a mid-stream disconnect + reconnect. This manifests as either a duplicated last token (client sees token N twice) or a missing token (client never receives token N). Affected: hosted API (prim...
text:
Summary: Several customers reported truncated or duplicated tokens at the very end of Server-Sent Events (SSE) streams after a mid-stream disconnect + reconnect. This manifests as either a duplicated last token (client sees token N twice) or a missing token (client never receives token N). Affected: hosted API (primary), some Dedicated deployments behind our proxy layer, rare on Private when customers use older SDKs.

Observed during escalations: two enterprise customers (redacted) saw hallucination/regression in downstream parsers caused by duplicated tokens; a PLG customer reported lost final punctuation on short-generation use cases. These were reproducible in the lab by simulating a TCP reset during the final flush of the last chunk.

Root cause hypothesis: a race between the runtime's chunk flush (server side) and the resume-token/ack state machine. When an SSE connection closes ...

title: Weighted-donation failover for quota limiter with prefetch leases
preview:
Summary:
We need a robust mechanism to handle limiter outages and cross-region failovers that preserves SLOs and fairness during bursts. Current behavior: when a regional limiter is unhealthy or restarts, requests either queue up and spike synchronously at failover targets or are dropped inconsistently, causing cust...
text:
Summary:
We need a robust mechanism to handle limiter outages and cross-region failovers that preserves SLOs and fairness during bursts. Current behavior: when a regional limiter is unhealthy or restarts, requests either queue up and spike synchronously at failover targets or are dropped inconsistently, causing customer-visible latency and unfair token consumption across tenants. Multi-region failovers sometimes cause duplicate quota allocation or starvation depending on which limiter hears the request first.

Proposal (high level):
1) Prefetch leases: limiters can issue short-duration 'leases' to upstream routing and edge proxies that represent a guaranteed allowance of N tokens/requests for a specific key (org/token). Leases are cheap bearer tokens that are valid for a small TTL (e.g., 2s) and do not require synchronous coordination for every request.
2) Weighted donation on recover...

title: Standardize retry/timeout/backoff behavior and idempotency across services & SDKs
preview: Problem statement: disparate retry/timeout/backoff implementations across microservices and language SDKs are causing unpredictable tail latency and duplicate side-effects during multi-region failovers. We need a cross-team standard that: 1) defines default retry counts, backoff strategies, and timeouts tuned for ou...
text: Problem statement: disparate retry/timeout/backoff implementations across microservices and language SDKs are causing unpredictable tail latency and duplicate side-effects during multi-region failovers. We need a cross-team standard that: 1) defines default retry counts, backoff strategies, and timeouts tuned for our SLO targets; 2) mandates idempotency key semantics for mutating endpoints; 3) provides opt-in hedged request support for latency-sensitive routes; and 4) integrates with circuit breakers and load-shedding policies for overload protection. Scope: core HTTP/gRPC services, Redwood SDKs (JS, Python, Go), and gateway/proxy layer. Out of scope: third-party integrations and legacy internal admin tooling (handled in follow-ups).

title: Private incremental upgrade catalog and policy engine
preview: Implement an incremental upgrade catalog + policy engine for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem) that drives safe, multi-stage upgrades and schema-aware migrations. The system will encode compatible upgrade paths, policy rules for gated rollouts, and produce a dry-run/rollback rehearsal plan that operators can execute bef...
text: Implement an incremental upgrade catalog + policy engine for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem) that drives safe, multi-stage upgrades and schema-aware migrations. The system will encode compatible upgrade paths, policy rules for gated rollouts, and produce a dry-run/rollback rehearsal plan that operators can execute before hitting customer environments.

title: Investigate HPA/KEDA sensitivity drift causing under-scaling during warm-start bursts
preview: Summary: On 2026-03-10 we observed repeated under-scaling of model serving pools during multiple short warm-start bursts for the text-generation fleet in us-west-2. Symptoms were elevated 95th and 99th percentile latency spikes while overall CPU/GPU utilization remained well below autoscale thresholds. The effect ma...
text:
Summary: On 2026-03-10 we observed repeated under-scaling of model serving pools during multiple short warm-start bursts for the text-generation fleet in us-west-2. Symptoms were elevated 95th and 99th percentile latency spikes while overall CPU/GPU utilization remained well below autoscale thresholds. The effect manifested as degraded tail latency for a subset of customers (~2% of production traffic) for ~25 minutes.

What happened (brief): A change in KEDA scaling behavior combined with a drift in the HPA metric window exposed a gap where rapid bursts of new sessions (warm-starts that populate KV cache) caused transient token-generation latency to spike, but the scaling system did not react quickly enough due to the scaler's debounce/windowing configuration and a higher-than-expected metric noise floor. Our existing safeguards (minReplicas, fallback routing) did not fully prevent la...

title: Revoked API key reintroduced after deployment rollback, causing temporary privilege resurfacing
preview: Customer-reported incidents across Dedicated and Private deployments: several customers observed that API keys previously revoked were briefly accepted again following a control-plane deployment rollback. Impact window ~5-20 minutes depending on region and edge cache TTL. This is a high-severity auth bypass affectin...
text: Customer-reported incidents across Dedicated and Private deployments: several customers observed that API keys previously revoked were briefly accepted again following a control-plane deployment rollback. Impact window ~5-20 minutes depending on region and edge cache TTL. This is a high-severity auth bypass affecting token/API-key revocation guarantees. Affected surfaces: API key auth in gateway, session reconciliation on dedicated pools, and private control planes with local cache replicas.

title: Parallel trace writer pool + adaptive backpressure to reduce span-ingest tail latency
preview: Problem: trace ingestion pipeline shows intermittent high tail latency (p99 export spikes >1s) caused by single-threaded export writer, unbounded queueing, and allocation-heavy span encoding. Goal: implement a parallel writer pool with bounded per-worker queues and adaptive backpressure to stabilize p99 latency and ...
text: Problem: trace ingestion pipeline shows intermittent high tail latency (p99 export spikes >1s) caused by single-threaded export writer, unbounded queueing, and allocation-heavy span encoding. Goal: implement a parallel writer pool with bounded per-worker queues and adaptive backpressure to stabilize p99 latency and reduce allocation pressure.

title: Multi-tenant Prefix Bloom Index and Lazy Merge for GPU KV Sharing
preview: Introduce a lightweight multi-tenant prefix membership index (Bloom-based) and a lazy merge workflow to enable high-confidence cross-request prefix deduplication without expensive eager KV consolidation. Goal: increase effective KV cache reuse across sessions and tenants while reducing GPU slab fragmentation and pea...
text: Introduce a lightweight multi-tenant prefix membership index (Bloom-based) and a lazy merge workflow to enable high-confidence cross-request prefix deduplication without expensive eager KV consolidation. Goal: increase effective KV cache reuse across sessions and tenants while reducing GPU slab fragmentation and peak memory pressure for continuous-batching workloads.

title: Throughput certification runbook for Dedicated capacity (benchmark harness & acceptance tests)
preview: Create an operational runbook and test artifacts to certify Dedicated capacity SLAs and throughput guarantees. The goal is a repeatable, customer-facing acceptance test suite and an internal benchmark harness that measures sustained and burst throughput, autoscaling behavior, and graceful degradation across model va...
text: Create an operational runbook and test artifacts to certify Dedicated capacity SLAs and throughput guarantees. The goal is a repeatable, customer-facing acceptance test suite and an internal benchmark harness that measures sustained and burst throughput, autoscaling behavior, and graceful degradation across model variants and instance types. This ticket covers the benchmarking methodology, harness implementation notes, accepted measurement tools, and concrete acceptance criteria for customer handoff.

title: GPU slice gateway and tenant quota sentinel
preview: Problem: Dedicated customers are experiencing unpredictable tail latency and throughput degradation when noisy tenants temporarily consume disproportionate GPU bandwidth. Current coarse hard-quotas and tenant lane reservation designs either underutilize fleet capacity or cause abrupt preemptions that break customer ...
text:
Problem: Dedicated customers are experiencing unpredictable tail latency and throughput degradation when noisy tenants temporarily consume disproportionate GPU bandwidth. Current coarse hard-quotas and tenant lane reservation designs either underutilize fleet capacity or cause abrupt preemptions that break customer SLAs.

Goal: Implement a lightweight admission-and-control plane ("GPU Slice Gateway") plus a tenant quota sentinel service that enforces temporal throughput guarantees, provides graceful degradation, and drives autoscaling triggers with predictive noisy-neighbor suppression. The solution should prioritize tenant separation, minimize noisy neighbor impact, and allow transparent quota accounting for reporting and billing.

Scope: Implement control-plane components (gateway + sentinel), runtime enforcement hooks (cgroup/ML runtime pacing integration), telemetry & signals for ...

title: Router guardrail: threshold-gating and automated cutover for region/tier/model variants
preview: Design and implement a router-level guardrail that gates traffic based on automated thresholds (429 spikes, p99 latency timeouts, request error-rate) and performs an automated cutover to safe model variants or alternate regions/tiers when thresholds are breached. This ticket covers policy semantics, signal aggregati...
text: Design and implement a router-level guardrail that gates traffic based on automated thresholds (429 spikes, p99 latency timeouts, request error-rate) and performs an automated cutover to safe model variants or alternate regions/tiers when thresholds are breached. This ticket covers policy semantics, signal aggregation, fast-path circuit gating, and a gradual rollback/cutover orchestration to avoid oscillation and customer-visible flapping.

title: Cold-to-Warm Ramp & Eviction Interplay benchmark for KV prefix cache
preview: Purpose: quantify how cold-to-warm traffic ramps interact with eviction policy and memory budget to affect tokens/sec, p95 latency, and cache hit-rate for the KV prefix cache. The goal is to produce an automated benchmark/harness that covers steady-state and transient ramp scenarios representative of real Redwood wo...
text: Purpose: quantify how cold-to-warm traffic ramps interact with eviction policy and memory budget to affect tokens/sec, p95 latency, and cache hit-rate for the KV prefix cache. The goal is to produce an automated benchmark/harness that covers steady-state and transient ramp scenarios representative of real Redwood workloads (bursty PLG onboarding, background workers, multi-tenant spikes). This ticket covers harness design, a hardware sweep across instance shapes, baseline capture, and a regression detector that raises alerts when p95 or tokens/sec degrade beyond defined thresholds.

title: Quantile-driven stream adaptive scheduler for heterogeneous turn lengths
preview: Background: Production traffic exhibits heavy heterogeneity in prompt vs response lengths: many short prompt-only calls interleaved with long-generation responses. Our current continuous-batching admission and KV-reservation heuristics optimize for average token density and suffer in two ways: 1) p99 latency spikes ...
text:
Background: Production traffic exhibits heavy heterogeneity in prompt vs response lengths: many short prompt-only calls interleaved with long-generation responses. Our current continuous-batching admission and KV-reservation heuristics optimize for average token density and suffer in two ways: 1) p99 latency spikes when long turns monopolize slots and fragment GPU slab memory, and 2) sustained KV-residency increases memory fragmentation, reducing effective batch capacity and increasing per-token cost.

Goal: Implement a quantile-driven stream admission controller and per-cohort KV-reservation adaptor that dynamically adjusts admission thresholds and KV retention targets to reduce p99 latency and lower token-costs for mixed prompt/response workloads without degrading p50 throughput. This ticket covers design, prototype, evaluation on synthetic and shadow traffic, and an incremental rol...

title: Telemetry-driven Canary Policy Engine with Safety Slicing and Auto-Remediation
preview:
Summary:
Build a policy engine for canary rollouts that uses fine-grained safety 'slices' (cohorts defined by traffic attributes) combined with telemetry-driven feedback to adapt step schedules, traffic splits, and automatic remediation (rollback or mitigation). This ticket scopes the runtime config language, evalua...
text:
Summary:
Build a policy engine for canary rollouts that uses fine-grained safety 'slices' (cohorts defined by traffic attributes) combined with telemetry-driven feedback to adapt step schedules, traffic splits, and automatic remediation (rollback or mitigation). This ticket scopes the runtime config language, evaluator, guardrail triggers, and integration points with Console telemetry and rollout API.

Goals:
- Provide a compact declarative rollout spec that supports: staged traffic splits, cohort-based overrides, shadow probing, and adaptive step extension or rollback based on live telemetry.
- Implement a safety-slicing system to define cohorts (by tenant, geolocation, request-size, prompt-hash buckets, model-variant) and enforce per-slice SLO/thresholds.
- Build a feedback loop: live telemetry drives step duration, step size, and triggers (pause/rollback/mitigation).
- Surface deci...

title: Private packaging experiment: split license + ops-credit model, tiered support SLAs, and scheduled upgrade windows with SQ streamlining
preview: Background: Enterprise prospects evaluating Private/VPC or on-prem deployments frequently request predictable TCO, clear upgrade cadence, and a scoped security questionnaire (SQ) turnaround. Current packaging conversations stall on three fronts: (1) license fee framing (flat annual vs usage-adjusted commit), (2) ope...
text:
Background: Enterprise prospects evaluating Private/VPC or on-prem deployments frequently request predictable TCO, clear upgrade cadence, and a scoped security questionnaire (SQ) turnaround. Current packaging conversations stall on three fronts: (1) license fee framing (flat annual vs usage-adjusted commit), (2) operational support entitlements and response SLAs, and (3) upgrade/patch cadence commitments for air-gapped customers.

Objective: Run a six-week experiment to validate a modular packaging construct that (a) splits the offering into Base License + Ops Credits, (b) defines three support tiers with concrete SLA/RPO/RTO and add-on expedited patching, and (c) commits to a scheduled upgrade window approach (quarterly minor + semiannual major) with an emergency patch path. Concurrently, streamline enterprise SQ responses by producing an SQ playbook and a checklist that reduces lega...

title: Backcompat contracts and automated rollout validators across runtime, API, SDKs, Console
preview: Goal: Define machine-readable backwards-compatibility contracts per product surface (runtime, public API, SDKs, Console) and implement a set of automated rollout validators that run as pre-flight checks in CI and as runtime canary guards. This ticket covers the contract schema, the validator rules, a CLI pre-flight ...
text: Goal: Define machine-readable backwards-compatibility contracts per product surface (runtime, public API, SDKs, Console) and implement a set of automated rollout validators that run as pre-flight checks in CI and as runtime canary guards. This ticket covers the contract schema, the validator rules, a CLI pre-flight tool (redwood-upgrade-check), and CI/console integration for gating upgrades on Dedicated/Private/Hosted channels.

title: SSO group-merge race causing mixed allow/deny and intermittent permission-denied for admin users
preview: Summary: Several enterprise customers reported intermittent 'permission denied' errors for accounts that should have retained admin privileges after SSO group synchronizations. Issue occurs when IdP group membership changes and our SSO connector performs a concurrent group merge/update while RBAC v2 is reconciling g...
text:
Summary: Several enterprise customers reported intermittent 'permission denied' errors for accounts that should have retained admin privileges after SSO group synchronizations. Issue occurs when IdP group membership changes and our SSO connector performs a concurrent group merge/update while RBAC v2 is reconciling grants. The race produces a transient state where allow rules from one source are applied but deny rules from another are not fully reconciled, leaving an effective deny for certain admin actions.

Reproduction notes:
- Steps to reproduce observed in prod test: 1) User A is in IdP Group 'Org-Admins' and a local RBAC role maps that group to 'org_admin' with full permissions. 2) During a bulk group update from the IdP (group rename/merge across OUs), the SSO connector issues overlapping add/remove events. 3) The RBAC v2 comparator merges deltas out-of-order: remove(GroupX)->ap...

title: Privilege slicing and hierarchical resource model with enriched audit context
preview: Design and deliver the RBAC v2 role model covering: a compact hierarchical resource identifier scheme, fine-grained scope types (invoke/read/write/admin), role composition semantics, and an audit-event schema that supports downstream SIEM and compliance exports. This ticket consolidates decisions for engineering imp...
text: Design and deliver the RBAC v2 role model covering: a compact hierarchical resource identifier scheme, fine-grained scope types (invoke/read/write/admin), role composition semantics, and an audit-event schema that supports downstream SIEM and compliance exports. This ticket consolidates decisions for engineering implementation and provides migration guidance from the v1 flat-permission model. The goal is a high-performance, cache-friendly authorization API that preserves strong auditability and supports SSO/SAML mapping and SCIM provisioning.

title: Dedicated cluster: noise detection and dynamic reservation adjustments
preview: Follow-up initiative after recent dedicated cluster capacity incidents. Build a detection pipeline for noisy-neighbor patterns and an adaptive reservation adjustment workflow that reduces both tail latency spikes and wasted reserved capacity. Focus on automated detection + short-term mitigations and a phased rollout...
text: Follow-up initiative after recent dedicated cluster capacity incidents. Build a detection pipeline for noisy-neighbor patterns and an adaptive reservation adjustment workflow that reduces both tail latency spikes and wasted reserved capacity. Focus on automated detection + short-term mitigations and a phased rollout of dynamic reservation resizing and adaptive throttling policies.

title: Auto-restore coordinator for risky config flips and batching/caching policy changes
preview: Goal: Build an automated 'restore coordinator' that detects harmful config flips (batching parameters, KV/prefix cache toggles, quantization profiles, autoscale knobs) and performs safe, auditable restore actions with minimal user intervention. The system will combine econometric signals (per-token cost delta), SLO ...
text:
Goal: Build an automated 'restore coordinator' that detects harmful config flips (batching parameters, KV/prefix cache toggles, quantization profiles, autoscale knobs) and performs safe, auditable restore actions with minimal user intervention. The system will combine econometric signals (per-token cost delta), SLO degradation detectors (p50/p95 latency, error-rate), and semantic regressions from lightweight evals to decide whether to restore previous config state. Key requirements: 1) low false positive restores, 2) explainable decision logs, 3) safe execution with canary/shadow simulation prior to any live restore, 4) reconciliation so long-running requests and KV cache state are consistent after a restore.

Scope: initial MVP targets hosted and Dedicated controls for batching/window-size and KV-cache enable/disable; later phases will add quantization profile flips, autoscaler polic...

title: Orbit Release Sentinel: Safe versioned rollouts and traffic modulation for Hosted API
preview: Problem: Our Hosted API deployment pipeline currently lacks a unified mechanism to (1) publish versioned model/runtime artifacts to LTS/edge channels, (2) enforce automated rollout gates that consider regional SLOs and cost tiers, and (3) perform incremental traffic modulation between blue/green pairs with safe auto...
text:
Problem: Our Hosted API deployment pipeline currently lacks a unified mechanism to (1) publish versioned model/runtime artifacts to LTS/edge channels, (2) enforce automated rollout gates that consider regional SLOs and cost tiers, and (3) perform incremental traffic modulation between blue/green pairs with safe automated fallback. This has led to manual cutovers, ad-hoc regional throttles, and one incident where a skewed rollout caused increased 95p latency for a key EU customer.

Objective: Implement an "Orbit Release Sentinel" — a release-engineering component and associated runbook that manages version metadata, channel tagging (canary / stable / lts), per-region rollout gates, and traffic-modulation primitives (attenuate, reflect, abort) integrated with our runtime, routing layer, and console. The sentinel must support automated policy evaluation, staged traffic shifts, SLO-driven...

title: Multischema mux: decoder regression and streaming checkpoint validation suite
preview: Create an automated regression and smoke test suite that validates JSON mode enforcement and constrained-decoder behavior when a single request may negotiate different schema versions or switch schema mid-stream due to tool/function calls. The suite must cover: (1) streaming partial JSON that must be reassembled and...
text: Create an automated regression and smoke test suite that validates JSON mode enforcement and constrained-decoder behavior when a single request may negotiate different schema versions or switch schema mid-stream due to tool/function calls. The suite must cover: (1) streaming partial JSON that must be reassembled and validated at checkpoints, (2) tool-return payloads that introduce a different schema version and trigger dynamic schema selection, (3) constrained decoding enforcement under token-level penalties and sampling noise, and (4) compatibility fallbacks where the runtime substitutes a compatible schema adapter. Tests should run in CI against the latest decoder and the compatibility shim. Acceptance criteria: deterministic pass/fail for defined cases, clear failure classifications (decoder, schema-mapper, assembler, or tooling), and a small reproduction harness for local dev that...

title: Schema evolution and rehydration for compressed structured logs with correlation expansion
preview:
Context:
We are ingesting increasingly dense structured logs from edge sidecars using a lossless compression + compact schema header. This increases throughput and reduces storage cost, but it makes ad-hoc queries, cross-tenant correlation, and historical reindexing difficult when schemas evolve. Recent customer deb...
text:
Context:
We are ingesting increasingly dense structured logs from edge sidecars using a lossless compression + compact schema header. This increases throughput and reduces storage cost, but it makes ad-hoc queries, cross-tenant correlation, and historical reindexing difficult when schemas evolve. Recent customer debug sessions showed missing correlation fields when older compressors omitted optional correlation arrays.

Problem:
- Existing ingestion path stores a compressed payload blob with a compact schema id and field map. Query-time expansion requires the exact schema mapping; when schema evolves (field renames, optional arrays), queries return incomplete or missing 'corr_uid' and related join keys.
- Rehydration for observability workflows (alerts, slow-query traces) often needs field-normalized logs (expanded JSON) for Loki/ES indexing.
- Current migration attempts are manual an...

title: Admin command graph sandboxifier for safe runbook experimentation
preview: Build a lightweight 'sandboxifier' that compiles admin runbooks and ad-hoc operator sequences into an executable command graph that can be safely dry-run, validated, and replayed in an isolated environment. The goal is to let SREs and support engineers iterate on runbook changes, test permission/effect assumptions, ...
text: Build a lightweight 'sandboxifier' that compiles admin runbooks and ad-hoc operator sequences into an executable command graph that can be safely dry-run, validated, and replayed in an isolated environment. The goal is to let SREs and support engineers iterate on runbook changes, test permission/effect assumptions, and gather deterministic telemetry before committing changes to production playbooks. This work touches admin utilities, runbooks-as-tasks, and support automation surface area: command translation, idempotency checks, credential scoping, and replay telemetry.

title: Conductorlet: admin proxy to translate runbooks into idempotent tasklets
preview:
Background:
Redwood on-call and support teams maintain canonical runbooks in a mixture of Markdown and YAML playbooks. Operators frequently need to run gated, idempotent steps from those runbooks in a repeatable way while preserving audit logs, RBAC checks, and telemetry for cost/latency impact.

Problem:
Current ad...
text:
Background:
Redwood on-call and support teams maintain canonical runbooks in a mixture of Markdown and YAML playbooks. Operators frequently need to run gated, idempotent steps from those runbooks in a repeatable way while preserving audit logs, RBAC checks, and telemetry for cost/latency impact.

Problem:
Current ad-hoc scripts and manual runbook execution cause variability: missing telemetry, inconsistent retries, and manual permission escalation. We need a small translator/proxy that enables runbook-as-task execution across admin tooling (CLI, Console, and API) while enforcing safety gates and emitting structured telemetry.

Proposal:
Build \"Conductorlet\" — a lightweight admin proxy service that: 1) ingests a constrained runbook YAML fragment and compiles it to typed, idempotent tasklets; 2) applies RBAC and approval policies; 3) routes tasks to the runlet execution runtime (exist...

title: Route Adjudicator: incremental policy compiler and fallback-variant safety suite
preview: Problem: The current routing policy codebase mixes validation, normalization, and runtime compilation in a single path which makes small changes risky and causes subtle regressions when introducing fallback model variants or new region affinity rules. We also lack an automated way to detect when a policy change will...
text:
Problem: The current routing policy codebase mixes validation, normalization, and runtime compilation in a single path which makes small changes risky and causes subtle regressions when introducing fallback model variants or new region affinity rules. We also lack an automated way to detect when a policy change will route traffic to an incompatible model variant (e.g., quantized variant without KV cache support), or when tier routing inadvertently starves lower-priority tiers during burst windows.

Goal: Implement a Route Adjudicator: an incremental policy compiler that separates parsing, typed-IR generation, and runtime contract checking. Deliver a fallback-variant safety suite that runs both static checks and lightweight runtime emulation to verify compatibility across: model capabilities, kv-cache expectations, quantization constraints, and regional deployment affinity. Output shou...

title: Quantization profile lifecycle: FP8/INT8 calibration, canary rollout, and automated fallback policy
preview: Goal: Add a robust lifecycle for quantization profiles (FP8 and INT8) that ties automated calibration and accuracy validation into a safe canary rollout with deterministic automatic fallback when quality checks fail. Context: Teams want to ship quantized model variants to reduce cost while avoiding regressions in us...
text:
Goal: Add a robust lifecycle for quantization profiles (FP8 and INT8) that ties automated calibration and accuracy validation into a safe canary rollout with deterministic automatic fallback when quality checks fail. Context: Teams want to ship quantized model variants to reduce cost while avoiding regressions in user-facing metrics. We need a repeatable flow: calibration -> offline validation -> canary traffic -> live metric gating -> automated rollback/fallback to higher-precision variant if quality drops.

Scope:
- Define canonical quantization profile metadata and storage format for FP8/INT8 (including per-layer, per-op config and calibration seed).
- Implement a calibration job template (batchable) that produces candidate profiles and calibration stats (KL histograms, per-tensor max, activation ranges).
- Create an accuracy validation harness that runs candidate profiles against ...

title: Chronal credit trading preemptor with adaptive KV compaction
preview: Introduce a new preemption policy (Chronal Credit Trading Preemptor) that assigns time-slice credits to inflight requests based on SLO slack and tenant weight, and allows soft-trading of credits between slices. Integrate this with an adaptive KV compaction/eviction component that considers expected reuse probability...
text: Introduce a new preemption policy (Chronal Credit Trading Preemptor) that assigns time-slice credits to inflight requests based on SLO slack and tenant weight, and allows soft-trading of credits between slices. Integrate this with an adaptive KV compaction/eviction component that considers expected reuse probability to reduce GPU memory pressure while preserving hot KV residency. The goal is to improve 95/99p latency SLOs under memory pressure and to provide a smoother fairness curve across tenants while limiting destructive KV churn.

title: Lane escrow for throughput handover and quota reconciliation
preview: Introduce a tenant lane escrow mechanism that reserves short-duration throughput credits, enables graceful handover between autoscaler modes, and provides an auditable quota reconciliation ledger to mitigate noisy neighbor effects and enforce throughput guarantees on Dedicated capacity.
text: Introduce a tenant lane escrow mechanism that reserves short-duration throughput credits, enables graceful handover between autoscaler modes, and provides an auditable quota reconciliation ledger to mitigate noisy neighbor effects and enforce throughput guarantees on Dedicated capacity.

title: Call Schema Annotation Runtime and Diagnostic Envelope
preview: Define and implement a runtime annotation format and diagnostic envelope for function/tool calling that produces deterministic validation results, human- and machine-readable error messages, and graceful compatibility shims across older SDKs and model variants.
text: Define and implement a runtime annotation format and diagnostic envelope for function/tool calling that produces deterministic validation results, human- and machine-readable error messages, and graceful compatibility shims across older SDKs and model variants.

title: Observability library modernization and sampling/rate-limit performance cleanup
preview: Goal: Reduce p99 observability overhead and improve signal fidelity by modernizing our tracing and logging libraries across the runtime and gateway services. Scope: (1) Upgrade OpenTelemetry SDK to latest stable release and migrate from in-house shim to upstream semantic conventions; (2) Replace legacy logging wrapp...
text: Goal: Reduce p99 observability overhead and improve signal fidelity by modernizing our tracing and logging libraries across the runtime and gateway services. Scope: (1) Upgrade OpenTelemetry SDK to latest stable release and migrate from in-house shim to upstream semantic conventions; (2) Replace legacy logging wrapper with structured JSON logger (compatible with our central ingest); (3) Implement adaptive sampling and coordinated rate-limit hooks to reduce burst CPU in high-throughput tails; (4) Standardize metric naming and delta-counters to avoid cardinality explosion. Out of scope: agent-side end-to-end tracing of third-party adapters (followup ticket).

title: Probabilistic Traffic Scheduler for Model Experiments
preview: Create a lightweight A/B testing scheduler that allocates user traffic probabilistically across model versions and supports sequential early-stopping, shadow lanes, and automatic rollback. The goal is a product-safe experiment primitive that engineers and platform teams can launch from the Console with deterministic...
text: Create a lightweight A/B testing scheduler that allocates user traffic probabilistically across model versions and supports sequential early-stopping, shadow lanes, and automatic rollback. The goal is a product-safe experiment primitive that engineers and platform teams can launch from the Console with deterministic exposure guarantees, integrated statistical checks, and production-friendly observability. This ticket defines the experiment configuration, exposure caps, statistical tests, early-stop rules, rollback triggers, and Console UX hooks.

title: SDK adoption bellwether cases and triage rubric for enterprise client libraries
preview: Create a reproducible triage rubric and a small set of "bellwether" SDK/features to prioritize customer requests for language support, auth patterns, and streaming ergonomics. The goal is to reduce stove-piped ad-hoc requests, surface measurable signals for engineering investment, and provide clear guidance for Solu...
text: Create a reproducible triage rubric and a small set of "bellwether" SDK/features to prioritize customer requests for language support, auth patterns, and streaming ergonomics. The goal is to reduce stove-piped ad-hoc requests, surface measurable signals for engineering investment, and provide clear guidance for Solutions/SEs during enterprise onboarding.

title: Tiered commitment lanes and cutline simulator for API / Console / Runtime
preview: Objective: Define a decision framework and a lightweight simulator that models staggered release lanes (Canary, Tier A reserved, Public) and enforces scope-lock / cutline criteria across API, Console, and Runtime services. Purpose is to reduce last-minute scope churn, standardize cross-team signals for go/no-go, and...
text:
Objective: Define a decision framework and a lightweight simulator that models staggered release lanes (Canary, Tier A reserved, Public) and enforces scope-lock / cutline criteria across API, Console, and Runtime services. Purpose is to reduce last-minute scope churn, standardize cross-team signals for go/no-go, and produce a canonical release notes payload for comms and docs teams.

Scope:
- Model three release lanes with configurable thresholds: latency P95 delta, error-rate increase, backend capacity headroom, and customer-impact flags.
- Capture stepwise cut criteria (feature freeze, doc freeze, last-call rolloffs) and map to owners.
- Provide a simple simulator (spreadsheet + runbook) to answer \"if metric X breaches at T-minus 3 days, which features cut and what comms runbook executes?\"
- Produce a release-notes skeleton and per-lane comms checklist that legal, support, and sal...

title: Token-surge priority-queue starvation & asymmetric circuit-breaker tripping postmortem
preview: Summary: On 2026-02-18 a sustained set of long-input requests originating from a single large customer (tokenization spike with many long sequences) caused priority queue starvation in the us-west-2 serving fleet. This produced asymmetric circuit-breaker tripping in secondary regions, uneven load shedding, and a 20-...
text: Summary: On 2026-02-18 a sustained set of long-input requests originating from a single large customer (tokenization spike with many long sequences) caused priority queue starvation in the us-west-2 serving fleet. This produced asymmetric circuit-breaker tripping in secondary regions, uneven load shedding, and a 20-minute p99 SLO breach for hosted generation endpoints. This postmortem documents the timeline, root cause, mitigations applied during the incident, and follow-up action items to prevent recurrence.

title: Session-affinity precedence and safe rollout coordination for version pins across region/tier fallbacks
preview: Problem: As we roll out model versions (canary/A-B) and allow session or user-level model pins, overlapping policy sources (admin pins, session pins, cohort assignment, geo/tier routing) create ambiguity about which model variant should serve a request when capacity or region constraints require fallback. This ticke...
text:
Problem: As we roll out model versions (canary/A-B) and allow session or user-level model pins, overlapping policy sources (admin pins, session pins, cohort assignment, geo/tier routing) create ambiguity about which model variant should serve a request when capacity or region constraints require fallback. This ticket defines deterministic precedence rules, session-affinity TTL semantics, and a safe degrade strategy that preserves rollout guarantees (cohort ratios) while enforcing region/tier constraints and minimizing customer-visible regressions.

Goal: Produce an implementable spec and integration tests for the routing resolver that enforce: (1) clear precedence among pin sources; (2) TTL-based session pin decay and re-assignment algorithm; (3) cohort preservation under geo/tier fallback (weight-preserving degrade); (4) automatic rollback thresholds and kill-switch integration; (5) ...

title: Design and implement dynamic scaling + throttling for embeddings and reranking paths
preview: Problem: embedding and reranking traffic patterns have diverging latency and throughput requirements. Short reranking requests (many small vectors + similarity ops) can congest the embedding pool and trigger expensive cold-scaling events. Current autoscaler reacts to GPU utilization with high oscillation and no per-...
text:
Problem: embedding and reranking traffic patterns have diverging latency and throughput requirements. Short reranking requests (many small vectors + similarity ops) can congest the embedding pool and trigger expensive cold-scaling events. Current autoscaler reacts to GPU utilization with high oscillation and no per-route prioritization, causing latency spikes for P0 customers and unexpected cost.

Goal: implement a deterministic, service-level aware scaling and throttling policy that: (1) separates capacity planning for embeddings vs reranking jobs, (2) adds adaptive queueing with backpressure and rate-limit tiers, (3) exposes config knobs for priority lanes and burst allowances, and (4) produces measurable improvements in p95/p99 latency and cost per embedding unit.

High-level approach:
- Introduce two logical request lanes inside the embedding service: "fast" (low-latency, low-batc...

title: Runtime path instability: capacity-latch causing p95 latency spikes and follow-ups
preview: During a weekend capacity surge on mixed-quant inference workloads we observed repeated p95/p99 latency spikes and sustained throughput degradation. The runtime entered a pathological state we are calling a 'capacity latch' where the scheduler consistently favored a slow fused-attention kernel variant for a subset o...
text: During a weekend capacity surge on mixed-quant inference workloads we observed repeated p95/p99 latency spikes and sustained throughput degradation. The runtime entered a pathological state we are calling a 'capacity latch' where the scheduler consistently favored a slow fused-attention kernel variant for a subset of requests, causing microbatch pileup and eviction churn. This ticket contains the incident summary, reproduction notes, root-cause analysis, immediate mitigations applied, and prioritized action items to prevent recurrence.

title: Reduce 5xx waves by isolating long-lived streaming sessions and adjusting SDK retry semantics
preview: Summary: Multiple enterprise customers reported intermittent 5xx bursts and elevated tail latency during peak hours. Investigation found a pattern where long-lived streaming sessions (websocket/streaming RPCs) from a small set of tenants were holding front-end connection slots and preventing efficient backpressure o...
text:
Summary: Multiple enterprise customers reported intermittent 5xx bursts and elevated tail latency during peak hours. Investigation found a pattern where long-lived streaming sessions (websocket/streaming RPCs) from a small set of tenants were holding front-end connection slots and preventing efficient backpressure on the serving runtime. Combined with aggressive client retries and lack of per-tenant concurrent admission limits at the edge, this triggered connection pile-ups that led to proxy saturations, kernel backpressure in the runtime, and cascading 502/504 responses for unrelated tenants.

Impact: Several customers (hosted and dedicated) experienced degraded throughput and elevated error rates for ~2–8 minute windows. Customer escalations received from 3 enterprise accounts; one had partial feature outages in production.

Root cause (multifactor):
- Front door / edge allowed unbo...

title: Staged deployment failure hypothesis log and safety upgrades
preview: After the 2026-02-02 staged rollout of semantic-sim-2.1 to hosted customers, we observed a regression that escaped canary checks and resulted in elevated hallucination rates and increased token error responses. This ticket collates hypotheses from the on-call and post-incident review, captures agreed runbook and saf...
text: After the 2026-02-02 staged rollout of semantic-sim-2.1 to hosted customers, we observed a regression that escaped canary checks and resulted in elevated hallucination rates and increased token error responses. This ticket collates hypotheses from the on-call and post-incident review, captures agreed runbook and safety changes, and tracks follow-ups required to reduce blast radius for future staged deployments.

title: Ops Kiosk: microflow kiosk and contextual quick-fix executor
preview: Build a lightweight, searchable ops kiosk UI that exposes a curated set of small, idempotent microflows (pre-approved repair steps) for on-call and support engineers. The kiosk is intended to shorten mean-time-to-repair for common issues while preserving safety via RBAC, dry-run previews, and automatic audit logs.
text: Build a lightweight, searchable ops kiosk UI that exposes a curated set of small, idempotent microflows (pre-approved repair steps) for on-call and support engineers. The kiosk is intended to shorten mean-time-to-repair for common issues while preserving safety via RBAC, dry-run previews, and automatic audit logs.

title: Time-bound delegation: ephemeral role provisioning and automated access-evidence pipeline
preview: Problem: Customers require demonstrable, time-scoped access controls for auditors and internal security reviews across hosted and private deployments. Current RBAC and SCIM flows support static roles and periodic manual reviews; we lack a standardized mechanism for ephemeral (time-bound) delegations, automated SCIM ...
text: Problem: Customers require demonstrable, time-scoped access controls for auditors and internal security reviews across hosted and private deployments. Current RBAC and SCIM flows support static roles and periodic manual reviews; we lack a standardized mechanism for ephemeral (time-bound) delegations, automated SCIM lifecycle reconciliation for temporary grants, and an evidence export that ties sessions/approvals to attestation artifacts. Goal: Implement ephemeral role provisioning (JIT-style TTL grants), SCIM extensions for temporary memberships, SSO/SAML approval hooks for delegations, and an evidence pipeline that produces auditable exports linking request, approver, grant duration, and revocation events. This ticket covers design and core implementation for infra + control plane components required for rollout to Dedicated and Private customers.

title: Tokenization-aware latency buckets and SLO crediting telemetry
preview: Problem: token-level latency SLIs currently assume a 1:1 token cost and uniform tokenization across models and regions. In practice, different tokenizers and model families result in widely varying token counts for the same semantic request. This skews per-token SLI calculation, SLO burn rates, and makes cross-model...
text:
Problem: token-level latency SLIs currently assume a 1:1 token cost and uniform tokenization across models and regions. In practice, different tokenizers and model families result in widely varying token counts for the same semantic request. This skews per-token SLI calculation, SLO burn rates, and makes cross-model/regional SLO comparisons noisy.

Goal: implement instrumentation that buckets latency by effective tokenization granularity and emits SLO-crediting metrics that normalize for tokenizer differences. This will: 1) provide accurate per-request SLO credit accounting for multi-model routes; 2) enable regional SLO policies to account for tokenizer-induced variance; 3) improve attribution when circuit breakers/firewalls trigger load-shedding by isolating tokenization effects from serving-path latency.

Scope: telemetry only (no changes to circuit-breaking behavior). Produce dashb...

title: Probe timing & KEDA cooldown interaction caused scale-down cascade and large cold-start spike
preview: Summary: On 2026-03-05 at ~03:42 UTC we observed a production incident affecting the inference request surface for our chat-routing service. Symptoms included a sudden spike in 5xx errors and 99th percentile latency for ~22 minutes for customers routed to region eu-west-1. The root cause was an interaction between m...
text: Summary: On 2026-03-05 at ~03:42 UTC we observed a production incident affecting the inference request surface for our chat-routing service. Symptoms included a sudden spike in 5xx errors and 99th percentile latency for ~22 minutes for customers routed to region eu-west-1. The root cause was an interaction between misconfigured Kubernetes probes, an init-container that delayed readiness export of request metrics, and KEDA's scaler cooldown/stabilization settings which led to a rapid cascade of scale-downs to zero followed by slow scale-up (cold starts). This ticket documents the timeline, technical cause, immediate mitigations, and long-term action items to prevent recurrence.

title: Kernel Heuristics Latency-Fairness Consolidator
preview: Build a consolidated heuristic layer and lightweight autotuner that chooses kernels per-request to balance latency fairness across customers and sequence-length distributions. Unlike existing per-arch or per-seqlen rules, this consolidator combines short-term microbenchmark probes, online bandit signals, and histori...
text: Build a consolidated heuristic layer and lightweight autotuner that chooses kernels per-request to balance latency fairness across customers and sequence-length distributions. Unlike existing per-arch or per-seqlen rules, this consolidator combines short-term microbenchmark probes, online bandit signals, and historical seqlen/arch fingerprints to produce a latency- and cost-aware kernel choice with safe fallbacks for tail avoidance.

title: Request lifecycle anchor and KV stabilizer for async worker propagation
preview: Introduce a lightweight request lifecycle anchor header and a stable KV-mapping strategy to ensure spans and baggage survive boundary crossings through internal queueing systems, batchers, and long-running worker pools. This ticket implements SDK middleware and server-side request decorators to create a deterministi...
text: Introduce a lightweight request lifecycle anchor header and a stable KV-mapping strategy to ensure spans and baggage survive boundary crossings through internal queueing systems, batchers, and long-running worker pools. This ticket implements SDK middleware and server-side request decorators to create a deterministic anchor ID, attach compact KV-stabilizer metadata, and rehydrate trace context in asynchronous consumers while preserving sampling fidelity.
